title: GETTING GOOD RESTFUL SLEEP | HG AMITASANA DAS | GITA LIFE Live

have chosen a very very important topic and the discussion is going to be on sleep.
As we all agree, sleep is a very very integral part of our lives.

one-fourth of our lives
between one-fourth and one-third of our lives we spend sleeping

Lord Shri Krishna shared.
prayers in the Bhagavad Gita. There are many verses where Krishna is talking about sleep.

nice wonderful session with lot of takeaways, lot of learnings emerging from this particular

The two verses where first verse Krishna talks about one who eats too much or eats too less,

So in this verse Krishna is directly talking about the importance of sleep, regulation,

One who eats too much or does not eat enough, one who sleeps too much or does not sleep

There is another verse where Krishna says one who is regulated in his eating, sleeping,

practicing this yoga system.
Again, regulation, one who is regulated in his eating, sleeping, recreation and work.
As I mentioned earlier, we all need good quality sleep.
We all need good quality sleep.
Good quality sleep for us to work efficiently, for us to have optimal health, lot of people

sleep invariably will also have challenges with respect to their alertness, their ability
to work efficiently, even with their health issues.
So, sleep is very important, although we know that when we come to Krishna consciousness,
we want to minimize the number of hours we sleep because we understand that we have lot

Time is very precious.
We all have limited time.
Life is, human life is very precious.
Before we take our last breath, we have lot of important things to accomplish, to accomplish

In that sense, in that sense, we can see that sleep is a necessary evil because we have

So, what is that optimal?
The optimal level of sleep where, yes, we get the required rejuvenation for the body

Lot of issues, lot of people are not able to sleep unless they do not have the proper

Many people having issues like insomnia.
So, let us discuss what is the optimal number of hours a person should sleep in 24 hours.

The age when children, when the child, the childhood, maybe the number of hours of sleep

When a person becomes old, the requirement for sleep will increase.
So a lot of research was done on this particular subject.
So some research threw up the results like okay, we need minimum of seven to eight hours.
Some researchers came up with a number of hours.
Some researchers came up with a number of hours.
Some researchers came up with a number of hours.

There is a lot of, you know, different researchers are throwing up different results.
In fact, there have been a lot of conferences held on this particular topic.
And a lot of people have debated on this subject.

No doubt, we need six to seven hours of sleep.
But today, a lot of people are talking about it is not the number of hours, it is not the

The number of hours that we have to worry about or strive for is not the quantity of

If a person gets five hours of deep, good, restful sleep, it is much better than seven
hours or eight hours of sleep where he is tossing and turning in the bed and, you know,
mind is racing.
He has not got a deep, restful sleep.
That is why you would have seen many times.
We also, when we wake up in the morning, even after seven hours of sleep, eight hours of
sleep, we are not fresh, we feel groggy, we feel tired.
So it is important that we learn or we practice, we mould our lives, we follow certain principles,
follow certain regulations.
So that
we get good quality sleep, deep restful sleep.
That is very important.
No doubt, the number of hours is also important, but relatively speaking, it is secondary.

They would sleep for few hours.
Actually, Prabhupada, he would sleep for three or four hours, he would get up at one o'clock

He would sleep for barely three to four hours.
So it is possible that we can reduce the number of hours.
But if we are
having good control over our mind, our thoughts, our senses and we are able to get good deep
restful sleep, then we can manage because at the end of the day body needs time to rejuvenate.
Sleep is a time when body rejuvenates, re-energizes itself.
So we should not just blindly go for the number of hours of sleep, understand why sleep is
important for the body and get good quality sleep.
That should be our desired aspiration or goal.
Before we get into what we can do, what regulations we can follow, it is very important for us
to understand a concept called sleep cycle.
Many people are not aware of this understanding of sleep cycle.
When we go to rest in the night, when we sleep, we go through different stages of sleep.
It is not that we just go to sleep and then there is a constant, we have deep sleep and
then morning when the alarm rings or when we want to wake up, we wake up.
There are different stages of sleep and as I said, many people have done research on
this.
So they have figured out.
There are different ways of measuring the brain waves when we are sleeping, just like
heartbeat can be measured, heart rate can be measured.
So when we go to bed, we go through a stage which is between wakeful stage and deep sleep
stage.
That is called stage one, where the number of cycles per second, a brain frequency at
which the brain, the neurons vibrate, reduces.
And then we get into stage two sleep, which is between stage one and stage three which
is deep sleep stage.
Deep sleep stage or stage three is a time when actually all the growth hormones, all
the repairing work.
sending signals to various parts of the body, getting signals back from the various parts,
because ultimately assuming that a person is, you know, some things have to be repaired,
he has got a cut in his body, signals have to go back and forth. Just like I was thinking,
just like when airplane comes back and lands, it has to go to a hangar for some repairs
and that is a time when all the, you know, the engineers work on the plane, figure out
where it has gone wrong, what parts have gone faulty, removing those parts, replacing those parts
and then the plane comes back out of the hangar and then it can be used for, you know, for its normal
operations. So, then we have deep sleep which is called
stage three and this is where actually the stage three is the most important part where
the body gets rejuvenated.
And then we have again back to stage two and then we have REM, rapid eye movement where
this is a stage where a person will most likely get some dreams.
This is when a person tosses
and turns in the bed and then again a person goes through the next cycle. So, these cycles,
we go in multiple cycles in a particular night, REM, stage one, stage two, deep sleep, stage two,
REM, again back to stage two, stage three which is deep sleep and we go to multiple cycles.
Now, many people have faced this problem.
They keep an alarm, they have decided the previous night that I will get up
at 5 30 a.m or whatever the time may be
or 6 a.m and when the alarm rings we somehow force ourselves out of the bed
and even if we have slept for six hours, seven hours, assuming that previous night I have slept
on time.
And got good six hours of sleep, six and a half hours of sleep.
I wake up and the alarm rings, somehow push myself out of the bed.
Quite possible that we will feel very groggy, very sleepy.
If we are chanting we are not able to chant properly,
not able to concentrate and one might wonder what mistake have I done? I have slept for six and a half
hours.
And I have got a face,
but I have slept only six hours.
And I have had a very good quality of sleep.
I did not wake up in the night, what went wrong?
The answer is, if you have woken up when you were in a stage three or deep sleep stage
and suddenly because of alarm, you woke up, then you will feel groggy you will feel completely,
so to say, uncomfortable, you will feel very drowsy, you will feel like go back and sleep,
irrespective of number of hours you have slept.
So therefore, it is said the best stage when a person wakes up is REM stage, rapid eye
movement.
That is the time when a person is almost awake.
So in a particular, the typical sleep cycle, again it varies from person to person, it
is around, around 90 minutes.
It varies, for somebody it could be 70 minutes, 80 minutes, it could be 110 minutes.
It is good that we complete some number of cycles and not wake up in between a cycle,
more so when we are in deep sleep stage.
So let us say a person has woken up in REM stage after 3 cycles of sleep, he will be
more fresh than a person who has woken up in deep sleep stage, stage 3, after having
completed 4 cycles of sleep.
Now it is a very tricky question.
So how do we figure out?
How do we wake up?
When?
We are in a REM stage or stage 1, after having completed one cycle.
That is why it is very, very important if you check, you know, on Google or YouTube
how to get good quality sleep, invariably you will get a suggestion that be very disciplined
as far as your sleep timings are concerned.
Sleep as far.
As far as possible sleep at the same time and get up at the same time every day, including

Lot of people think Sunday is a day where we can, you know, catch up with the rest and
sleep more, does not matter.
But what happens is, we have a biological clock, a rhythm.
Assuming, let us say a person sleeps at 10 o'clock.
10 o'clock.
And gets up at 5 o'clock.
And suddenly the next day a person sleeps at 10.35 and again tries to get up at 5 o'clock.
It will be a big mess.
Because 10 o'clock to 5 o'clock, assuming the body has got used to and then suddenly
sleeps at 10.30, invariably at 5 o'clock he would be in middle of a sleep cycle.
So, without getting too much into technical details about sleep cycle, by the way, there

When you wake up.

Power nap is where a person sleeps for 20 minutes or 20 to 25 minutes.
This is when the body gets into stage 1 sleep and has not got into stage 2 and stage 3 deep

And when a person wakes up after in a stage 1 sleep, he feels relaxed and regenerated.
He has got some sleep but he has not got into deep sleep where to wake up suddenly he
will feel groggy and tired.
That is again sleep cycle understanding, helps us understand what is power nap.

If we are used to the snooze button option in the alarm clock, wake up time is the time

And the battle is between our mind and mattress.
Suddenly at that point, the mind comes up with different reasons, different thoughts,
how it is better for you to sleep for another 10 minutes, 20 minutes, it will be more relaxing,

Some article you have read about why it is important to have six hours, seven hours sleep.
That article suddenly will pop up in the mind.
Suddenly a person will feel, no, I'm still tired, I need more rest.
And at times we switch off the alarm and suddenly find ourselves waking up one and half hours

And the whole day, it is very difficult.
is gone. Then throughout the day we are struggling to catch up with our chanting, catch up with all

from the bed.
At least to switch off the alarm, we have to physically get out of the bed. This helps.
Now if we all have something called as personal integrity account,
just like we have a bank account, there is some debits and credits. When we deposit the money
in our bank account, there is a credit. When we withdraw the money, there is a debit.
And then we have a bank balance. Anytime we take a printout of our bank account,
pass sheet or whatever, you will see a balance which will have passbook will show
some debits, some credits and the final net balance in our bank account.
All of us have something called as personal integrity account. That means amount of trust
we have on ourselves.
When we make a resolve or a commitment, not only at what time we will wake up for any other thing,
when we keep up that commitment, there is a deposit in our personal integrity account.
Our confidence in ourselves, in our ability to make and keep promises increases.
There is a deposit in our personal integrity account.
And, whenever we make a commitment and do not keep up that commitment, when we say that
I will get up at 5 am and I do not get up at 5 am, there is a withdrawal from my personal
integrity account.
In other words, over a period of time, if there is lot of withdrawals, the lot of commitments
which I have made and I am not able to keep up that commitment many, many times and the
withdrawals happen quite often, there could be a negative balance in my personal integrity
account.
A negative balance indicates that a person does not trust himself.
When a person, let us say, aspires to achieve a particular goal, he sets a goal that I want
to be this or do this or achieve this.
His mind will doubt, you cannot even get up at 5 am.
You think you will do this, you will be able to achieve this.
So we all have an internal chatter going on within our minds, thoughts keep producing.
So when a person keeps a commitment and, you know, as per the commitment, executes his
life, there are deposits in this personal integrity account.
So therefore, it is very important.
It is important that we get up because we have decided not that someone else has come

So it is good, not only from the point of view of early morning victory, also from the

We will start the day with victory.

It is the quality of sleep.
Having talked about quality, it is important for us to understand sleep cycle.
In other words, we have, we go through many cycles in a particular night, we should try

If we wake up by force, just by determination.

We may not be able to strictly follow a very disciplined lifestyle, but as far as possible,

As far as possible, we should try to sleep at the same time and wake up at the same time

At times when you have a very good body rhythm, then you do not need to sleep.

It is also important for your personal Integrity account.

I lose that battle between mind and matter.
then quite possible that we lose confidence on ourselves. So, we, our mind will start doubting
that this small simple thing you are not able to do, what you will do big big things in life. So,
we lose trust on ourselves. It is very important that we make and keep up our commitments and the

We are relatively undisturbed. The world hasn't woken up and we can do lot of like chanting,

time. We can engage in those spiritual activities. We can engage in those spiritual activities. We can

So, now coming to practical tips. First thing, it is very important that most of us here today
in this particular session may not be taking tea, coffee. If a person is taking, it is important
that we keep at least seven to eight hours of gap between our sleep time. Let us say a person
is sleeping at 9.30 or 10 p.m. You should avoid taking any kind of stimulants, nicotine, caffeine,
energy drinks, tea, coffee, at least 10 hours gap. That means from 12 o'clock or one o'clock
in the afternoon you should avoid because when this caffeine or nicotine is there in our blood
stream, it can disturb our sleep cycle. They are stimulants. They can disturb our sleep cycle.
So, keep that gap. Some people cannot avoid tea, coffee. They have got used to it. So, you can have
it probably in the morning. Best is to avoid. Best that we do not fall trap to any addictions, be it
very gross or subtle. Caffeine is also in one form. It is addictive.
Three hours before sleep. Let us say if a person sleeps at 10 p.m., three hours, they should be
on a minimum three hours, two and a half to three hours gap between our meal and the sleep time.
Now, there is a technical reason behind it. A lot of blood in our body is residing near the
gut area, the intestine area. And if we eat very close to our bedtime, sleep time,
a lot of energy in our body gets consumed in digesting the food even when we are sleeping.
So, that blood is unable to reach to the brain. That is why many of us
in the afternoon after having had a heavy lunch, we feel groggy and sleepy
because the blood is in that gut area, in the stomach area, aiding the digestion process.
So, when we have eaten quite late and slept, not kept that gap, then
there is not enough supply of blood to the brain. And as I mentioned, brain does a very,
very important function when we are sleeping to rejuvenate the body. A lot of signals go
from the brain back and forth to different parts of the body. It coordinates various
functions which are happening in the body. The organs are still functioning.
And that can get severely disrupted if we have eaten late. A lot of energy gets consumed in
digesting the food. And that is why even after you have slept for six hours, seven hours,
or if you have respected the sleep cycle which I talked about, quite possible that we will not
wake up refreshed. So, keep a three-hour gap. And this has been practiced. If you see
our elders in the family, they were, you know, very close to sunset time,
they were having their meals. Now times have changed. People, you know, sleep late and eat late,
have very late dinners. That has resulted in many lifestyle diseases.
But otherwise, it is best that we have our last meal before sunset, which is seven o'clock. And even
